title: trifolium echinatum subsp. carmelii
intro: Trifolium echinatum subsp. carmelii, also known as Carmel clover or prickly clover, is a subspecies of clover that belongs to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, Trifolium echinatum subsp. carmelii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
